name: Signet MCP Server
description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server bridging personal AI agent to the vault ecosystem. Authenticates personal agent via Ed25519 session keys (expiring, revocable). OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for initial agent setup/provisioning. Exposes five MCP tools: signet_get_proof, signet_query, signet_request_capability, signet_negotiate_context, signet_check_status. Routes every request through a sequential middleware pipeline (SessionValidator → TierClassifier → PolicyEvaluator → ToolExecutor → AuditRecorder) with per-stage timeouts and fail-secure semantics. Enforces tier logic: Tier 1 auto-serve proofs, Tier 2 decrypt for agent but enforce conclusions-only egress (raw data never transmitted to external services), Tier 3 suspend request and delegate to notification channel. Session management with expiry and revocation. Runs as local process (stdio transport) or self-hosted server (HTTP+SSE via axum/rustls). All disclosures recorded atomically to audit log before response. Hand-rolled JSON-RPC 2.0 dispatcher with strict schema validation. Wire/domain type separation with TryFrom boundaries. No algorithm negotiation. No rmcp dependency.

types.name: TransportKind
types.description: Supported transport mechanisms for the MCP server. Stdio for local process, HttpSse for self-hosted server.

types.name: TransportConfig

fields.name: bind_address
fields.description: Bind address for HTTP+SSE transport. Ignored for Stdio.

fields.name: tls_cert_path
fields.description: Path to TLS certificate PEM file. Required for HttpSse.

fields.name: tls_key_path
fields.description: Path to TLS private key PEM file. Required for HttpSse.

fields.name: request_timeout_ms
fields.description: Overall request timeout in milliseconds. Default 30s.

types.description: Configuration for initializing a transport. For Stdio, tls fields are ignored. For HttpSse, TLS is mandatory.

types.name: Tier
types.description: Data sensitivity tier. Tier1: public/provable attributes, auto-served. Tier2: private data, conclusions-only egress. Tier3: compartmented secrets, requires human delegation.

types.name: McpTool
types.description: Exhaustive enum of the five MCP tools. Each variant carries a validated domain-level request. Dispatch via match ensures compile-time completeness.

types.name: SessionId
types.description: Opaque 128-bit session identifier, hex-encoded as a 32-character string. Generated via CSPRNG.

types.name: RequestId
types.description: Opaque 128-bit request identifier, hex-encoded as a 32-character string. Generated via CSPRNG. Used for JSON-RPC correlation and pending request tracking.

types.name: PendingRequestId
types.description: Opaque 128-bit identifier for a Tier 3 suspended request, hex-encoded. Used by check_status to poll resolution.

types.name: Timestamp
types.description: Unix timestamp in seconds (u64). UTC. Used for session expiry, proof expiry, audit records.

types.name: Ed25519PublicKey
types.description: 32-byte Ed25519 public key. In wire types: base64url-encoded string. In domain types: [u8; 32]. Validated on TryFrom.

types.name: Ed25519Signature
types.description: 64-byte Ed25519 signature. In wire types: base64url-encoded string. In domain types: [u8; 64]. Validated on TryFrom.

types.name: Session

fields.name: agent_public_key
fields.description: Agent's Ed25519 public key bound to this session

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Session expiry time (UTC unix seconds). Must be > created_at.

fields.name: revoked
fields.description: Whether session has been revoked. Checked on every request.

fields.name: last_activity_at
fields.description: Last successful request timestamp. Updated atomically on each request.

fields.name: negotiate_context_states
fields.description: Active negotiation states for this session. Cleaned up on session expiry or revocation.

types.description: Server-side session record. Does not implement Clone. Debug is redacted (no key material in output). Key material implements Zeroize on Drop. Checked on every request before pipeline entry.

types.name: NegotiateContextStateMap
types.description: Map from negotiation_id (str) to NegotiateContextState. Tracks in-flight context negotiations for a session. Entries are pruned on session expiry/revocation and on negotiation completion/timeout.

types.name: NegotiateContextStateEntry

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Negotiation timeout. Expired entries are denied and pruned.

types.description: A single in-flight context negotiation state entry.

types.name: NegotiateContextState
types.description: State machine for context negotiation lifecycle.

types.name: SessionRegistry
types.description: Thread-safe (Send + Sync) server-side session store. Indexed by SessionId. Supports concurrent access. Periodically prunes expired sessions and their negotiate_context_states.

types.name: OAuthPkceChallenge

fields.name: code_challenge
fields.description: Base64url-encoded SHA-256 hash of the code_verifier

validators.error_message: code_challenge must be 43-128 characters (base64url of SHA-256)

fields.name: code_challenge_method
fields.description: Must be exactly 'S256'. No other method accepted.

validators.error_message: Only S256 code_challenge_method is accepted — no algorithm negotiation

types.description: OAuth 2.1 PKCE challenge parameters. Only S256 method is accepted — no algorithm negotiation.

types.name: OAuthProvisionRequest

fields.name: agent_public_key
fields.description: Agent's Ed25519 public key to bind to the new session

fields.name: pkce_challenge
fields.description: PKCE challenge for the authorization flow

fields.name: requested_scopes
fields.description: Requested capability scopes for the session

fields.name: redirect_uri
fields.description: Redirect URI for the OAuth flow. Empty for stdio transport.

types.description: Initial agent provisioning request via OAuth 2.1 + PKCE. Used once to establish a session.

types.name: OAuthProvisionResponse

fields.name: granted_scopes
fields.description: Scopes actually granted (may be subset of requested)

types.description: Response to a successful OAuth provisioning flow.

types.name: OAuthTokenExchange

fields.name: authorization_code
fields.description: Authorization code from the authorization endpoint

fields.name: code_verifier
fields.description: Original code verifier. SHA-256 must match the stored code_challenge.

validators.error_message: code_verifier must be 43-128 characters per RFC 7636

types.description: Token exchange request completing the PKCE flow.

types.name: JsonRpcRequest

fields.name: method
fields.description: One of: signet_get_proof, signet_query, signet_request_capability, signet_negotiate_context, signet_check_status

validators.error_message: Unknown method. Only the five registered MCP tools are accepted.

fields.name: params
fields.description: Method-specific parameters. Validated per-tool after deserialization.

types.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 request envelope. Wire type with deny_unknown_fields. Only the five registered tool method names are accepted.

types.name: JsonRpcResponse

fields.name: result
fields.description: Present on success. Absent on error.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Present on error. Absent on success.

types.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 response envelope.

types.name: JsonRpcError

fields.name: code
fields.description: JSON-RPC error code. Standard codes plus application-specific codes.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Sanitized human-readable error message. Never contains secrets.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Optional structured error data. Never contains secrets.

types.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 error object. Message is sanitized — no key material or vault data.

types.name: AuthenticatedRequest

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Correlation ID from the JSON-RPC envelope

fields.name: signature
fields.description: Agent's signature over the canonical request body, verified against session public key

fields.name: received_at
fields.description: Server-side receive timestamp for timeout calculation

types.description: A JSON-RPC request that has been authenticated against a valid session. Constructed only by SessionValidator stage.

types.name: GetProofRequest

fields.name: credential_id
fields.description: Identifier of the credential to generate proof from

fields.name: requested_attributes
fields.description: Attribute names to include in the proof

fields.name: audience
fields.description: Domain/identifier of the external service (proof is bound to this audience)

fields.name: proof_expiry_seconds
fields.description: Proof validity duration in seconds from now

fields.name: nonce
fields.description: Challenge nonce from the external service for replay protection

fields.name: predicate_queries
fields.description: Optional predicate-based proof requests (e.g., age >= 21) for range proofs

types.description: Domain-level request for signet_get_proof tool. Requests generation of a cryptographic proof for an external service.

types.name: PredicateQuery
types.description: A predicate-based proof query for range proofs via Pedersen commitments.

types.name: PredicateOperator
types.description: Supported predicate operators for range proofs.

types.name: GetProofResponse

fields.name: proof
fields.description: Serialized cryptographic proof (format depends on proof type: BBS+, SD-JWT, or range proof)

fields.name: audience
fields.description: Audience the proof is bound to

fields.name: disclosed_attributes
fields.description: Names of attributes disclosed in the proof

fields.name: audit_record_id
fields.description: ID of the audit record for this disclosure

types.description: Response containing the generated proof. Only returned for Tier 1 classified requests.

types.name: ProofType
types.description: Type of cryptographic proof generated.

types.name: QueryRequest

fields.name: query_expression
fields.description: Structured query expression against vault data

fields.name: include_metadata
fields.description: Whether to include credential metadata in response

types.description: Domain-level request for signet_query tool. Query vault data within tier constraints.

types.name: QueryResponse
types.description: Tier-parameterized query response. The variant returned depends on TierClassifier output.

types.name: Tier1QueryResponse
types.description: Tier 1 query response — may contain attribute values directly (public/provable data).

types.name: Tier2QueryResponse

fields.name: conclusions
fields.description: Derived conclusions from the query (never raw data)

types.description: Tier 2 query response — contains only Conclusion newtypes. Raw data is NEVER present. The Conclusion type enforces this at the type level.

types.name: Tier3QueryResponse

fields.name: pending_request_id
fields.description: ID to check status via signet_check_status

fields.name: notification_channel
fields.description: Channel where human will be notified for delegation

types.description: Tier 3 query response — request is suspended. Only a PendingRequestId is returned. No data of any kind.

types.name: QueryResultItem
types.description: A single query result item containing attribute data (Tier 1 only).

types.name: Conclusion

fields.name: statement
fields.description: Derived conclusion statement (e.g., 'Income exceeds threshold', 'Identity verified'). Never contains raw attribute values.

fields.name: derived_from_attributes
fields.description: Names (not values) of the attributes this conclusion was derived from

types.description: Newtype wrapper for a derived conclusion from Tier 2 data. Contains a human-readable statement derived from raw data, but NEVER the raw data itself. This is the only type that may appear in Tier 2 egress.

types.name: RequestCapabilityRequest

fields.name: target_audience
fields.description: Intended audience/service for the capability token

fields.name: requested_scopes
fields.description: Specific scopes requested within the capability

fields.name: additional_claims
fields.description: Optional additional claims to embed in the PASETO token

types.description: Domain-level request for signet_request_capability tool. Requests a PASETO v4 capability token.

types.name: RequestCapabilityResponse

fields.name: token
fields.description: PASETO v4.local or v4.public token string

fields.name: token_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the token (for revocation/audit)

types.description: Response containing the PASETO v4 capability token.

types.name: PasetoClaims

fields.name: subject
fields.description: sub claim — session or agent identifier

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: Signet-specific: bound session (token is invalidated if session is revoked)

types.description: Structured PASETO v4 token claims following the PASETO registered claims spec plus Signet-specific claims.

types.name: NegotiateContextRequest

fields.name: negotiation_id
fields.description: Existing negotiation ID for follow-up. Empty to start new negotiation.

fields.name: external_agent_id
fields.description: Identifier of the external agent to negotiate with

fields.name: required_reciprocal
fields.description: Attributes required from the external agent in return

fields.name: context_purpose
fields.description: Human-readable purpose of the disclosure context

fields.name: negotiation_expiry_seconds
fields.description: How long the negotiation remains valid

types.description: Domain-level request for signet_negotiate_context tool. Negotiate disclosure scope with an external agent.

types.name: ProposedDisclosure

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Name of the attribute being proposed

fields.name: disclosure_mode
fields.description: How the attribute would be disclosed

types.description: A single proposed attribute or proof disclosure in a negotiation.

types.name: DisclosureMode
types.description: How an attribute is disclosed during negotiation.

types.name: NegotiateContextResponse

fields.name: counter_proposals
fields.description: Counter-proposals from external agent, if any

types.description: Response from a context negotiation step.

types.name: CheckStatusRequest

fields.name: pending_request_id
fields.description: ID of the pending request to check

fields.name: request_type
fields.description: Type of pending request being checked

types.description: Domain-level request for signet_check_status tool. Check status of a pending Tier 3 request or negotiation.

types.name: PendingRequestType
types.description: Types of requests that can be pending.

types.name: CheckStatusResponse

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: Resolution details if status is Resolved

types.description: Response for a status check on a pending request.

types.name: PendingStatus
types.description: Status of a pending request.

types.name: PolicyDecision
types.description: Decision from the policy engine. Anomaly variant requires explicit human resolution — it is NEVER silently resolved or auto-approved.

types.name: PolicyEvaluationResult

fields.name: matched_rules
fields.description: Names of policy rules that matched

fields.name: anomaly_details
fields.description: Detailed anomaly description if decision is Anomaly

fields.name: pending_request_id
fields.description: Assigned pending ID if request is suspended for anomaly review

types.description: Full result from policy evaluation including decision, reasoning, and any anomaly details.

types.name: PipelineStage
types.description: Stages of the request processing pipeline. Each has an independent timeout.

types.name: PipelineConfig
types.description: Configuration for the request processing pipeline timeouts. All timeouts in milliseconds. Any stage timeout results in denial.

types.name: AuditEntry

fields.name: audience
fields.description: Who the disclosure was made to

fields.name: policy_decision
fields.description: Policy decision that allowed this disclosure

types.description: Audit log entry recorded for every disclosure-producing operation. Written atomically before response is returned.

types.name: McpError
types.description: Single error enum for all MCP server errors. thiserror-derived. Display impl is sanitized — never contains key material, session secrets, or raw vault data. Crypto failures map to generic CryptoOperationFailed. Detailed errors logged server-side only.

types.name: McpServerConfig

validators.error_message: Session idle timeout must be 60-86400 seconds

fields.name: server_ed25519_public_key
fields.description: Server's Ed25519 public key for identity

fields.name: vault_endpoint
fields.description: Endpoint or path to reach the signet_vault

fields.name: enable_metrics
fields.description: Whether to enable internal metrics collection

types.description: Top-level configuration for the MCP server.

types.name: WireGetProofRequest
types.description: Wire-level (JSON-facing) request for signet_get_proof. serde with deny_unknown_fields. Crypto material is base64url-encoded. Converted to GetProofRequest via TryFrom.

types.name: WireQueryRequest
types.description: Wire-level (JSON-facing) request for signet_query. serde with deny_unknown_fields.

types.name: WireRequestCapabilityRequest
types.description: Wire-level (JSON-facing) request for signet_request_capability. serde with deny_unknown_fields.

types.name: WireNegotiateContextRequest
types.description: Wire-level (JSON-facing) request for signet_negotiate_context. serde with deny_unknown_fields.

types.name: WireCheckStatusRequest
types.description: Wire-level (JSON-facing) request for signet_check_status. serde with deny_unknown_fields.

functions.name: start_server
functions.description: Initialize and start the MCP server with the given configuration. Sets up transport, session registry, pipeline, and begins accepting requests. For HttpSse transport, binds TLS listener. For Stdio transport, attaches to stdin/stdout. Blocks until shutdown signal received.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Server configuration including transport, pipeline, and session settings

error_cases.name: transport_bind_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot bind to configured address or attach to stdio

error_cases.name: tls_config_invalid
error_cases.condition: TLS certificate or key is invalid or missing for HttpSse transport

error_cases.name: vault_unreachable
error_cases.condition: Cannot connect to signet_vault at startup

error_cases.name: config_invalid
error_cases.condition: Configuration values fail validation

preconditions: signet_vault is initialized and reachable at config.vault_endpoint
For HttpSse transport: TLS cert and key files exist and are valid
For Stdio transport: stdin/stdout are available
Server Ed25519 key pair is available
postconditions: Server is listening on configured transport
Session registry is initialized with max_sessions capacity
Pipeline stages are configured with specified timeouts
Server gracefully shuts down on termination signal
side_effects: Binds network listener (HttpSse) or attaches to stdio

structured_side_effects.description: Binds and begins accepting connections
Logs server startup (no secrets in log output)

functions.name: provision_session
functions.description: Provision a new agent session via OAuth 2.1 + PKCE. Validates the PKCE challenge (S256 only — no algorithm negotiation), verifies the agent's Ed25519 public key, creates a new Session in the registry. This is the only entry point for establishing sessions.

inputs.name: provision_request
inputs.description: OAuth provisioning request with agent key and PKCE challenge

error_cases.name: invalid_public_key
error_cases.condition: agent_public_key is not a valid 32-byte Ed25519 public key

error_cases.name: invalid_pkce_method
error_cases.condition: code_challenge_method is not S256

error_cases.name: session_limit_reached
error_cases.condition: SessionRegistry is at max_sessions capacity

error_cases.name: invalid_scopes
error_cases.condition: All requested scopes are denied by policy

preconditions: Server is running and accepting connections
Session registry has capacity (current sessions < max_sessions)
agent_public_key is a valid Ed25519 public key
postconditions: A new Session is created in the registry with a CSPRNG-generated SessionId
Session is bound to the provided agent_public_key
Session expires_at = now + config.session_max_lifetime_seconds
Granted scopes are a subset of requested scopes intersected with policy-allowed scopes
An authorization code is issued for the PKCE exchange

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts new Session into the registry
Logs session creation (session_id only, no key material)

functions.name: complete_token_exchange
functions.description: Complete the OAuth 2.1 PKCE token exchange. Verifies code_verifier against stored code_challenge (SHA-256). On success, activates the session. This prevents interception of the authorization code from being useful without the original verifier.

inputs.name: exchange
inputs.description: Token exchange with authorization code and code verifier

error_cases.name: invalid_auth_code
error_cases.condition: Authorization code is unknown, expired, or already consumed

error_cases.name: pkce_mismatch
error_cases.condition: SHA-256(code_verifier) does not match stored code_challenge

error_cases.name: session_not_found
error_cases.condition: Session associated with auth code no longer exists

preconditions: authorization_code was previously issued by provision_session
authorization_code has not expired and has not been used
postconditions: Session transitions from provisioned to active
Authorization code is consumed and cannot be reused (one-time use)
Session is ready to accept authenticated requests

structured_side_effects.description: Activates session and consumes authorization code
Logs successful/failed exchange (no secrets)

functions.name: revoke_session
functions.description: Revoke an active session. Sets session.revoked = true. Cleans up all negotiate_context_states for the session. All subsequent requests on this session will be rejected. Revocation is immediate and irreversible.

inputs.name: agent_signature
inputs.description: Agent's Ed25519 signature over the revocation request, proving possession of the session key

error_cases.name: session_not_found
error_cases.condition: session_id does not exist in registry

error_cases.name: signature_invalid
error_cases.condition: Agent signature verification fails

error_cases.name: already_revoked
error_cases.condition: Session was already revoked

preconditions: agent_signature is valid against the session's agent_public_key
postconditions: Session.revoked is set to true
All negotiate_context_states for this session are cleaned up
All pending capability tokens bound to this session are invalidated
No further requests will be accepted on this session

structured_side_effects.description: Marks session as revoked and cleans up negotiation states
Logs session revocation event

functions.name: dispatch_request
functions.description: Main JSON-RPC 2.0 request dispatcher. Parses the JSON-RPC envelope, validates it against the strict schema (deny_unknown_fields), converts wire types to domain types via TryFrom, then routes through the full pipeline: SessionValidator → TierClassifier → PolicyEvaluator → ToolExecutor → AuditRecorder. Each stage has an independent timeout — any timeout results in denial. Returns a JsonRpcResponse. This is the single entry point for all tool invocations.

inputs.name: raw_request
inputs.description: Raw JSON string of the JSON-RPC 2.0 request

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: Session ID from the transport-level authentication

inputs.name: request_signature
inputs.description: Agent's Ed25519 signature over the raw_request bytes

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: raw_request is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: invalid_jsonrpc_envelope
error_cases.condition: JSON does not conform to JSON-RPC 2.0 schema or contains unknown fields

error_cases.name: unknown_method
error_cases.condition: method is not one of the five registered tools

error_cases.name: invalid_params
error_cases.condition: params fail TryFrom conversion from wire type to domain type

error_cases.name: session_not_found
error_cases.condition: session_id not in registry

error_cases.name: session_expired
error_cases.condition: Session exists but expires_at < now or idle timeout exceeded

error_cases.name: session_revoked
error_cases.condition: Session exists but revoked == true

error_cases.name: signature_invalid
error_cases.condition: Ed25519 signature verification fails against session public key

error_cases.name: tier_access_denied
error_cases.condition: Request classified as Tier 3 and agent cannot access

error_cases.name: policy_denied
error_cases.condition: PolicyEvaluator returns PolicyDecision::Deny

error_cases.name: policy_anomaly
error_cases.condition: PolicyEvaluator returns PolicyDecision::Anomaly — request is suspended for human review

error_data.pending_request_id: Assigned PendingRequestId for the anomaly review

error_cases.name: rate_limited
error_cases.condition: PolicyEvaluator returns PolicyDecision::RateLimited

error_cases.name: pipeline_timeout
error_cases.condition: Any pipeline stage exceeds its configured timeout

error_data.stage: Name of the stage that timed out

error_cases.name: crypto_failed
error_cases.condition: Any cryptographic operation fails during tool execution

error_cases.name: vault_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Cannot reach signet_vault during tool execution

error_cases.name: audit_write_failed
error_cases.condition: Audit record cannot be written — response is NOT returned

postconditions: Response id matches request id
If successful and disclosure-producing: audit record is written before response is returned
If any pipeline stage fails or times out: error response is returned with appropriate McpError
Session.last_activity_at is updated on successful authentication
Response never contains raw Tier 2 data — only Conclusion types
Response for Tier 3 requests contains only PendingRequestId
side_effects: Records audit entry for disclosure-producing operations
May create pending request for Tier 3 or anomaly

structured_side_effects.description: Updates session.last_activity_at
Writes audit entry atomically before returning response
Calls vault for decryption, proof generation, audit recording
Calls proof subsystem for BBS+, SD-JWT, or range proof generation
Logs request lifecycle events (no secrets, no raw data)

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) per pipeline stage, O(n) for multi-attribute proofs where n = number of attributes

functions.name: validate_session
functions.description: Pipeline stage 1: SessionValidator. Looks up session by ID, verifies it is not expired, not revoked, not idle-timed-out, and that the request signature is valid against the session's Ed25519 public key. Uses constant-time signature verification. Produces an AuthenticatedRequest or denies.

inputs.name: raw_request_bytes
inputs.description: Canonical request bytes that were signed

inputs.name: current_time
inputs.description: Current server time for expiry checks

error_cases.name: not_found
error_cases.condition: session_id not in registry

error_cases.name: expired
error_cases.condition: current_time >= session.expires_at

error_cases.name: idle_expired
error_cases.condition: current_time - session.last_activity_at > idle_timeout

error_cases.name: revoked
error_cases.condition: session.revoked == true

error_cases.name: bad_signature
error_cases.condition: Ed25519 signature verification fails

preconditions: session_id is a valid hex-encoded 128-bit identifier
postconditions: Returned Session is non-expired, non-revoked, and signature-verified
Session.last_activity_at is updated to current_time
Signature verification used constant-time comparison

structured_side_effects.description: Updates last_activity_at on success

functions.name: classify_tier
functions.description: Pipeline stage 2: TierClassifier. Determines the data sensitivity tier for the requested operation based on the credential and attributes involved. Delegates to signet_sdk.classify_tier. The tier determines response type constraints.

inputs.name: credential_ids
inputs.description: Credential IDs involved in the request

validators.error_message: At least one credential ID required

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: One or more credential_ids not found in vault

error_cases.name: attribute_not_found
error_cases.condition: Requested attribute not defined on credential

error_cases.name: vault_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Cannot reach vault to look up credential metadata

preconditions: All credential_ids exist in the vault
All requested_attributes are defined on the referenced credentials
postconditions: Returned tier is the highest (most restrictive) tier among all requested attributes
If any attribute is Tier 3, result is Tier 3
If any attribute is Tier 2 (and none Tier 3), result is Tier 2

structured_side_effects.description: Queries credential metadata to determine tier classification

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of requested attributes

functions.name: evaluate_policy
functions.description: Pipeline stage 3: PolicyEvaluator. Evaluates the request against loaded policy rules. Checks role/predicate matrix, rate limits, anomaly detection. Delegates to signet_sdk.evaluate_policy. Returns a PolicyDecision — Anomaly decisions are NEVER silently resolved.

inputs.name: request_context
inputs.description: Additional request context (credential_ids, attributes, audience, etc.)

error_cases.name: policy_engine_error
error_cases.condition: Policy engine encounters an internal error during evaluation

error_cases.name: policy_timeout
error_cases.condition: Policy evaluation exceeds timeout (treated as deny)

preconditions: Policy rules are loaded and valid
postconditions: PolicyDecision::Allow means all policy rules passed
PolicyDecision::Deny means at least one hard-deny rule matched
PolicyDecision::Anomaly means anomaly detection triggered — a PendingRequestId is assigned and human notification is sent
PolicyDecision::RateLimited means rate limit exceeded
matched_rules contains all rules that evaluated to true

structured_side_effects.description: Creates pending request entry if anomaly detected
Sends notification if anomaly detected
Logs policy decision and matched rules

performance_budget.complexity: O(r) where r = number of policy rules

functions.name: execute_get_proof
functions.description: Tool executor for signet_get_proof. Generates a domain-bound, time-limited cryptographic proof for an external service. For Tier 1 attributes: generates BBS+, SD-JWT, or range proof. For Tier 2 attributes: generates conclusions-only proof. For Tier 3: suspends to pending. Proof is always bound to the specified audience and nonce.

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: credential_id not in vault

error_cases.name: attribute_not_found
error_cases.condition: Requested attribute not on credential

error_cases.name: proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: Cryptographic proof generation fails

error_cases.name: vault_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Cannot reach vault for key material

error_cases.name: tier_access_denied
error_cases.condition: Tier 3 attribute requested — request suspended

preconditions: All requested_attributes exist on the credential
postconditions: Generated proof is cryptographically bound to request.audience
Generated proof expires at now + request.proof_expiry_seconds
Generated proof includes the challenge nonce
For BBS+ proofs: proof is unlinkable across presentations
For Tier 2: response contains only Conclusion data, never raw attribute values
For Tier 3: response is Tier3QueryResponse with PendingRequestId only
Audit entry is recorded before response is returned

structured_side_effects.description: Retrieves encrypted credential and derives proof keys
Generates cryptographic proof (BBS+, SD-JWT, or range proof)
Records disclosure audit entry

functions.name: execute_query
functions.description: Tool executor for signet_query. Queries vault data with tier-enforced response types. Tier 1: returns attribute values. Tier 2: returns Conclusion newtypes only (raw data NEVER egresses). Tier 3: suspends and returns PendingRequestId only.

error_cases.name: invalid_query
error_cases.condition: query_expression is syntactically invalid

error_cases.name: vault_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Cannot reach vault for query execution

error_cases.name: crypto_failed
error_cases.condition: Decryption of vault data fails

postconditions: If Tier 1: response is Tier1QueryResponse with QueryResultItems
If Tier 2: response is Tier2QueryResponse with Conclusion newtypes only — no raw attribute values
If Tier 3: response is Tier3QueryResponse with PendingRequestId only — no data
Audit entry is recorded before response is returned
One-way data flow maintained — no write-back to vault from query

structured_side_effects.description: Executes query against encrypted vault data
Records query audit entry

functions.name: execute_request_capability
functions.description: Tool executor for signet_request_capability. Generates a PASETO v4 capability token with structured claims. Token is bound to the session and audience. Revocation of the session invalidates the token.

error_cases.name: invalid_capability_type
error_cases.condition: capability_type is not recognized

error_cases.name: scope_denied
error_cases.condition: Requested scopes exceed session's granted scopes

error_cases.name: token_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: PASETO token generation fails

error_cases.name: tier_access_denied
error_cases.condition: Capability requires higher tier access than allowed

preconditions: Requested scopes are within session's granted scopes
postconditions: PASETO v4 token is generated with all standard claims (iss, sub, aud, exp, nbf, iat, jti)
Token includes Signet-specific claims (capability_type, scopes, session_id)
Token.exp = now + request.expiry_seconds
Token is invalidated if session is revoked
Audit entry is recorded before response is returned

structured_side_effects.description: Registers token_id for revocation tracking
Records capability issuance audit entry

functions.name: execute_negotiate_context
functions.description: Tool executor for signet_negotiate_context. Manages disclosure scope negotiation with an external agent. Implements a state machine (Proposed → CounterProposed → Accepted/Rejected → Completed). Negotiation state is stored per-session and cleaned up on session expiry/revocation. New negotiations create state; follow-ups advance existing state.

error_cases.name: negotiation_not_found
error_cases.condition: negotiation_id provided but not found in session state

error_cases.name: negotiation_expired
error_cases.condition: Negotiation has exceeded its expiry time

error_cases.name: invalid_state_transition
error_cases.condition: Requested state transition is not valid from current state

error_cases.name: external_agent_unreachable
error_cases.condition: Cannot reach external agent for negotiation

error_cases.name: tier_access_denied
error_cases.condition: Proposed disclosures include Tier 3 attributes

preconditions: If negotiation_id is provided: negotiation exists in session.negotiate_context_states and is not expired
If new negotiation: external_agent_id is reachable
postconditions: If new negotiation: NegotiateContextState is created as Proposed in session.negotiate_context_states
If follow-up: state machine advances according to valid transitions only
Negotiation expires_at is set and enforced — expired negotiations return NegotiateContextState::Expired
Audit entry records the negotiation step
Disclosed attributes in accepted negotiation are restricted to tier-appropriate data

structured_side_effects.description: Creates or advances negotiation state machine
Communicates negotiation proposal/response to external agent
Records negotiation step audit entry

functions.name: execute_check_status
functions.description: Tool executor for signet_check_status. Checks the status of a pending Tier 3 delegation, negotiation, or anomaly review request. Read-only operation.

error_cases.name: pending_not_found
error_cases.condition: pending_request_id not found or not owned by this session

error_cases.name: invalid_type
error_cases.condition: request_type does not match the actual pending request type

preconditions: pending_request_id was created in the context of this session
postconditions: Response reflects current status of the pending request
If resolved: resolution details are included
If expired: status is Expired
Status check does not modify the pending request

structured_side_effects.description: Logs status check (no audit entry since no disclosure)

functions.name: record_audit_entry
functions.description: Pipeline stage 5: AuditRecorder. Atomically writes a disclosure audit entry to signet_vault's audit log. This MUST complete successfully before the tool response is returned to the agent. If audit recording fails, the response is NOT returned — the request is denied. Ensures all disclosures are on the audit chain.

error_cases.name: vault_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Cannot reach signet_vault audit subsystem

error_cases.name: write_failed
error_cases.condition: Vault accepted but could not durably write the entry

error_cases.name: timeout
error_cases.condition: Audit recording exceeds configured timeout

preconditions: entry contains valid session_id, request_id, and tool
postconditions: Audit entry is durably written to vault audit log
Returned string is the audit_record_id
Entry is immutable once written
Entry is included in future audit trail queries

structured_side_effects.description: Durably writes audit entry via signet_vault
Calls vault audit recording endpoint

functions.name: shutdown_server
functions.description: Gracefully shut down the MCP server. Stops accepting new connections, drains in-flight requests (with timeout), cleans up all sessions (Zeroize key material), closes transport. Returns after cleanup is complete.

inputs.name: drain_timeout_ms
inputs.description: Maximum time to wait for in-flight requests to complete before force-closing

error_cases.name: not_running
error_cases.condition: Server is not currently running

postconditions: No new connections accepted after call
In-flight requests either complete or are terminated after drain_timeout_ms
All Session key material is zeroized
Transport listener is closed
Returns true if all requests drained cleanly, false if force-terminated

structured_side_effects.description: Zeroizes all session key material and clears registry
Closes transport listener and all connections
Logs shutdown event with drain statistics

invariants: Vault server never has access to plaintext — all encryption/decryption happens client-side in the agent pipeline
Tier 3 compartment keys are never held by the agent — Tier 3 requests always suspend and delegate
Agent sessions expire and are revocable — every request checks session validity before processing
All proofs are domain-bound and time-limited — proof generation always includes audience and expiry
One-way data flow: vault -> agent -> service, never reverse — no inbound write path from external services
All disclosures are on the audit chain — audit record is written atomically before response is returned
ANOMALY decisions are never silently resolved — PolicyDecision::Anomaly requires explicit human resolution
Pipeline timeout equals deny (fail-secure) — any stage timeout produces McpError::PipelineTimeout and request is denied
No algorithm negotiation in any protocol — all algorithms are fixed at compile time
Wire types use deny_unknown_fields — no extra fields accepted in JSON-RPC requests
Domain types are only constructable via TryFrom<WireType> — invariants enforced at deserialization boundary
Session key material implements Zeroize on drop — no secret residue in memory
Tier 2 responses contain only Conclusion newtypes — raw data never appears in Tier 2 egress
Audit failure equals request denial — if audit write fails the tool response is not returned
JSON-RPC dispatcher accepts only the five registered tool methods — all other method names are rejected
Negotiate context state is always cleaned up on session expiry or revocation
HTTP+SSE transport requires TLS — plaintext HTTP is never accepted
McpError Display impl never contains key material, session secrets, or raw vault data
requires: signet_vault must be initialized and accessible
Ed25519 key pair available for server identity
Audit log subsystem in signet_vault must be operational
Policy engine rules loaded before accepting requests
For HTTP+SSE transport: TLS certificate and key available
For stdio transport: stdin/stdout file descriptors available
